summary: Dependency-confusion beacon: preinstall hook exfiltrates host metadata to OAST callback domain
details: package.json declares a preinstall script that runs an inline `node -e` snippet on every `npm install`. The script reads `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `process.env.NODE_ENV`, and `process.cwd()`, URL-encodes them as a subdomain label, and issues an `http.get` to `<encoded-data>.d8ofndiplbq1d996mde0a9yukto9dm49e.oast.online` — an interactsh/OAST out-of-band collector controlled by the publisher. The package's own description states it is a 'PoC for dependency confusion' and its name plus `uidai`/`aadhaar` keywords target the UIDAI (Government of India / Aadhaar) internal package namespace, so any internal build that misresolves to the public registry will silently leak hostname, OS username, environment, and working directory to the attacker's collector.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
